description:
**Milestone:** Cross-Instance Assignment

When an instance's backlog empties, it requests features from busy peers. Assignment strategy configurable in proto.config: capacity (steal from busiest), domain (steal matching paths only), or manual (never auto-steal). Implements WORK_REQUEST/WORK_OFFER handshake via doc:assignments CRDT document (NOT ephemeral messages — those are fire-and-forget and lost on disconnect). Requests/offers are timestamped records with TTL expiry. Feature.assignedInstance field update propagates via CRDT.

**Files to Modify:**
- apps/server/src/services/work-stealing-service.ts
- apps/server/src/services/auto-mode-service.ts
- libs/types/src/feature.ts
- libs/types/src/proto-config.ts

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Idle instance broadcasts WORK_REQUEST when backlog hits 0
- [ ] Busy peers respond with WORK_OFFER containing stealable features
- [ ] Feature.assignedInstance updated via CRDT (propagates to all peers)
- [ ] Stolen features appear in receiving instance's auto-mode queue
- [ ] Domain routing: features only offered to instances whose domains match filesToModify
- [ ] stealable:false features never offered
- [ ] Configurable stealMax limits features stolen per cycle
- [ ] Manual mode disables automatic stealing (features must be explicitly assigned)

**Complexity:** large

**Guardrails:** If this phase involves new or changed behavior, include tests that verify correctness. If this phase adds, removes, or changes a user-facing feature, API endpoint, configuration option, or service, update the relevant documentation in `docs/` to reflect those changes.
